title: Prim's Algorithm
description: Given an undirected weighted graph with n nodes (0-indexed), find the total weight of the Minimum Spanning Tree using Prim's algorithm.
key_concept: Grow MST one edge at a time from a start node — always add the minimum weight edge connecting the MST to an unvisited node. Min-heap of (weight, node).
when_best: Dense graphs (better than Kruskal when E is close to V²), starting from a specific node

hints.L1: Start from node 0. Use a min-heap of (edge_weight, neighbor). Mark nodes as visited when added to MST.
hints.L2: Pop minimum from heap. If already visited, skip. Add weight to total, mark visited. Push all unvisited neighbors with their edge weights.
hints.L3: heap=[(0,0)]; visited=set(); total=0; while heap: w,u=heappop(heap); if u in visited: continue; visited.add(u); total+=w; for v,weight in graph[u]: if v not in visited: heappush(heap,(weight,v)); return total
